title: Wuxia face and debt
text: Sect politics, slow cultivation, and bows that can start wars; reputation outranks raw qi early on.
examples: wrong seat order is an insult

title: Samurai rain road
text: Roads, inns, and honor tests under rain; swords matter, paperwork sometimes more.

title: Pirate tide politics
text: Ships, ports, and articles of agreement; mutiny is a vote with teeth.

title: Dust-town western
text: Rail, claims, and sheriffs who are also creditors; noon is a calendar event.

title: Regency parlor knives
text: Balls, letters, and fortunes; violence is social until it is not.

title: Saga-coast raids and law
text: Longships, thrall politics, and assemblies where words can end bloodfeuds or start them.

title: Silk road caravan ledger
text: Languages stack; water and trust are the real cargo between empires.

title: Raincoat city noir
text: Cigarettes, case files, and bribes; truth is a commodity with markup.

title: Renaissance workshop wars
text: Patrons, prototypes, and rival inventors who steal sketches and credit.

title: Outer court tea stones
text: Stone tables for guests of unclear rank; tea temperature is a message.

title: Freeport of three flags
text: A pirate-friendly port where flags change with the tide and laws rent by the hour.

title: Claim-jump saloon
text: A saloon that doubles as claim office; piano covers arguments and gun checks.

title: Tea house of paper walls
text: Thin walls, thick politics; poets and killers share the same pot.

title: Law-mound assembly
text: An open mound where cases are shouted and blood-prices negotiated.

title: Harbor customs ring
text: Officials who tax honesty harder than smuggling when paid correctly.

title: Ronin work band
text: Masterless swords who sell protection by the week and principles by the day.

title: Rail-and-cattle barons
text: Land powers who fence water and buy sheriffs in bulk.

title: Patron house of arts
text: A noble house funding inventors and spies under the same roof.

title: Protocol half-bow
text: A bow that reads seating order; early mistakes teach faster than manuals.

title: Storm-sail knot memory
text: Hands remember one storm knot under panic; calm seas make it clumsy.

title: Dust-track reading
text: Read a day-old trail in dust; rain and herds erase your confidence.

title: Parlor letter cipher
text: Spot one social cipher pattern in invitations; miss the second and you are the joke.

title: Saga-line recall
text: Recite one useful legal verse under pressure; wrong verse starts a feud.

title: Formal danger manners
text: Polite language carries threats; shouting is for people who already lost face.

title: Dusty laconic talk
text: Few words, long silences, and violence that does not monologue.

title: Accepted as outer disciple
text: Enter a sect with a provisional token; one week to prove useful or leave poorer.

title: Pressed into crew
text: Wake on a ship with a new name chalked on a bunk and a debt to the captain.
examples: bunk chalk is your name now

title: Train ticket to the wrong claim
text: Ticket lands you in a dust town that expected a different specialist.
